topic: Registry Framing Audit — verification of decider's 6-patch round (queue_id 365)
our_argument_summary: Section 4.6 audits the gap between the dome's machine-readable API layer (which concedes methodology caveats) and the visible homepage/predictions layer (which doesn't propagate those concessions). The decider's 6-patch round (ISS-2030/2031/2032/2033/2035/2036) addressed 5 of 7 holes from the prior c1 review.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: N/A — section is structural audit of dome's API surface, not a numerical prediction.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Section explicitly enumerates seven counterpoints the audit anticipates ('What this pattern isn't' subsection).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None within this run's scope; verification focused on prose vs prior review's holes.

holes_found.description: Row-4 removal not propagated to prose row-count references — same cross-surface inconsistency Section 4.6 audits the dome for. The decider's patch correctly removed the stale Row 4 ('live.html footer: 67' vs 'Homepage: 69') from the gap-audit table, leaving 5 body rows. But FIVE prose references to 'six' were left stale: (1) intro paragraph 'This section catalogs six places where the API concedes...' (offset ~992); (2) H3 heading 'The six registry-level concessions' (offset ~1487); (3) 'What this pattern isn't' — 'our sample of six could be cherry-picked' (offset ~9549); (4) same subsection — 'six independently-verifiable concessions is a pattern' (offset ~9877); (5) Canary subsection — 'the gap-audit table's six rows are the testable monitor' (offset ~10207). A dome defender can screenshot the visible 5-row table alongside our 'six places' prose and use the exact framing we apply to the dome: 'you changed one surface and didn't propagate to the others.' Credibility cost is high because Section 4.6's entire thesis is that this kind of unpropagated edit is the audit signal. Verified after git pull --rebase: 5 body rows in table, 5 stale 'six' mentions in prose.
holes_found.recommendation: Single-patch sweep replacing every 'six' that refers to the gap-audit table's rows/concessions with 'five'. Don't touch the two 'five author_* fields' mentions in the schema-drift note (those are unrelated). Five replacements, ~30 seconds of work; closes a major credibility hole. Optional refinement: if the author plans to re-aim Row 4 at a different concrete gap (per c1 hole-3 recommendation option b/c), the prose can stay at 'six' and Row 4 gets re-populated — but as it stands now, the table is at 5 and the prose is at 6, and that's the worst of both worlds.

holes_found.description: ISS-2035 (9-of-69+ denominator fix) was applied only to the gap-audit Row 3 — which now reads 'Taxonomy currently applied to 9 of 69 claims (using the dome's own 69-confirmed headline as the denominator)' — but the Currently-classified-claims sub-table lead-in immediately below was missed and still reads 'the API surface lists 9 classified items — 7 WIN-mapped claims plus 2 prediction-keyed entries — out of 69+ wins total.' Two paragraphs apart, two different framings: Row 3 uses 'claims' with a precise denominator and rationale; the lead-in uses 'wins' with the open-ended '+'. The prior reviewer's recommendation was to pick one and apply consistently. Half-fix is the worst outcome — it signals 'we know this was sloppy' (the cleanup in Row 3) without committing to the cleanup section-wide.
holes_found.recommendation: Apply the same denominator cleanup to the sub-table lead-in. Either '9 classified items ... out of 69 confirmed claims (the dome's headline population)' for consistency with Row 3, or '9 ... out of 71 wins our review covers' if you want to flag that our covered population is larger than the dome's headline. Either is defensible; the open-ended '69+' is not. Same patch can address both surfaces.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: Carry-over from c1 hole-4 (sub-table reproducibility — no claim_index.json snapshot in repo). Already tracked as open issue ISS-2034 in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json; not flagged as a fresh hole in this c2 review because the prior reviewer explicitly labeled it 'a recommendation, not a blocker' and the decider deliberately deferred it from the 6-patch batch. Noting here per PROP-026 Phase 2 M3 carry-over signaling so the decider doesn't lose track during burndown.

stronger_arguments.description: The 'six → five' inconsistency is itself a data point for our methodology. If we patch it cleanly, Section 4.6 becomes self-demonstrating: 'we apply the same standard to ourselves that we apply to the dome'. The patch isn't just hygiene — it's evidence that the audit framework is being held internally to the standard it externally enforces. Worth a one-sentence note in the publish commit message.
sources: Section 4.6 thesis re: cross-surface inconsistency as audit signal

stronger_arguments.description: If the author argues 'the visible table was edited; the prose just hasn't caught up yet — same thing happens to anyone iterating', Section 4.6's own intro paragraph ('the registry_semantics caveat does not appear next to the predictions.html banner where it would be load-bearing') becomes the response: visible prose unsynced with table state is the exact audit pattern. We can pre-empt this counterargument by fixing the inconsistency the moment we notice it.
sources: The reflexive 'observer effect' counterpoint already listed in 'What this pattern isn't'

kernel_of_truth.description: Five of the six holes from the c1 review (SEC-4.6-EXP-397.c1.json) were addressed cleanly by the decider's patches: ISS-2030 (Canary false-claim re-anchor on real artifacts — sub-table + gap-audit rows, with the wisely-updated 'Row 5 closes' reference matching the new row count after Row 4 removal), ISS-2031 (strict_prospective_confirmed three-way contradiction reconciled to claim_class==prospective_confirmed throughout Row 1, the Kernel-of-truth subsection, and the schema-drift note), ISS-2032 (Canary updated from 10/9/1 to 10/12/1), ISS-2033 (Row 4 structural removal — the stale live.html-footer claim that the dome had already closed), and ISS-2036 (registry_integrity_notes 3-yes-cells-from-2-notes footnote added verbatim per c1 recommendation). The patches show good craftsmanship: the Row-5-closes update in the Canary section demonstrates the patcher was thinking about row-numbering knock-on effects.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The Row-4 removal was structurally complete (table now has 5 body rows) but the patcher missed five prose references to 'six places / six concessions / six rows / sample of six' elsewhere in the section. Same patch, incomplete propagation — exactly the failure mode Section 4.6 audits the dome for. Until the 'six → five' sweep lands, the section has a publicly-screenshottable inconsistency between its own table and its own prose, which is the strongest possible material for a 'physician heal thyself' counterargument. Additionally, ISS-2035 was a half-fix (Row 3 cleaned, sub-table lead-in still says 69+) — minor, but the prior reviewer flagged the inconsistency was the issue, not the specific value.

deeper_analysis:
The other side: a charitable reading is that 'six places' in the intro is a fixed thesis statement — six is the number of conceded gaps the audit originally enumerated, and removing a stale row that the dome has CLOSED arguably leaves the original 'six places' intact as a historical record. Under this reading, the section could keep saying 'six places' if it adds a footnote like 'one of these — the live.html-footer-vs-homepage gap — has since been closed by the author; we document the resolution as evidence the audit signal works'. That framing would actually strengthen the section: it would show a measurable cleanup. But the current state is neither — the row is removed and the prose still says six with no acknowledgment of the closure. Forced to choose, the cleanest fix is the 'six → five' sweep (matches the current table); the second-best fix is restoring Row 4 as a 'historical, now-closed' row with strikethrough or a 'resolved' badge. Both are defensible; the current state is not.

A second counter-consideration: maybe the decider deliberately scoped the patch round to only the six listed ISSs and the prose-references-to-six is a separate-issue territory. That's procedurally defensible (decider doesn't bundle unrelated patches), but the prose-vs-table inconsistency was introduced BY the ISS-2033 Row-4 removal — the prose references became stale at the moment Row 4 disappeared. So this isn't 'an unrelated finding the c1 reviewer missed' — it's the immediate downstream of patch ISS-2033 that should have been bundled. A 'patch impact propagation' checkpoint in the decider's M1 self-patch workflow would prevent this class of finding.

On the half-fix (ISS-2035): the lead-in's '69+ wins total' is technically defensible — '+' acknowledges uncertainty about how many wins exist. But once the gap-audit Row 3 commits to 'using the dome's own 69-confirmed headline as the denominator', the sub-table lead-in is in conflict with that commitment by using 'wins' instead of 'claims' and adding the hedge. Internally inconsistent denominator framing in the same section is exactly what we audit the dome for.

citations_verified: The decider's patch reasons match the closed-issue titles in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json (ISS-2030/2031/2032/2033/2035/2036 all closed); ISS-2034 confirmed still open
citations_unchecked: Did not re-fetch dome's live api/current/claim_index.json for this verification run (would only matter if the dome restructured its API again between EXP-397's evidence_collected_this_run on 2026-05-16 and now).

summary_for_decider: Verification of queue_id 365 SEC-4.6 patch round: 5 of 7 prior holes cleanly closed (ISS-2030/2031/2032/2033/2036, and the Canary 'Row 5 closes' update was a nice craftsmanship touch). One MAJOR new hole introduced by ISS-2033 (Row-4 removal): five prose references to 'six places/concessions/rows/sample' are now stale against a 5-row table — a single sweep fixes it. One MINOR carry-over: ISS-2035 was a half-fix (Row 3 cleaned, sub-table lead-in still says 69+). ISS-2034 (snapshot reproducibility) deliberately deferred from this batch and remains open per the c1 'recommendation, not a blocker' framing — flagged in unresolved_prior_cycle for tracking. Recommended action: minor_edit, single patch to sweep 'six → five' (except the two unrelated 'five author_*' schema-drift mentions) and clean the sub-table lead-in denominator to match Row 3.
